# Figure description: 2_Карта_фактов_N-42-XXXIII.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/N-42-XXXIII/2_Карта_фактов_N-42-XXXIII.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **"Карта фактического материала"** (Map of Actual/Factual Material) — a data-coverage or survey-point distribution map. This type of figure is standard in Soviet-era geological reports and documents the spatial distribution of all field observations, measurements, and sampling/drilling points used in the compilation of the geological survey. It is not a geological interpretation map but rather a record of where data was collected.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet:** N-42-XXXIII (printed prominently at top)
- **Scale:** 1:200,000 (confirmed by both the text and the scale bar at bottom)
- **Year:** 1981 г. (1981)
- **Issuing organization:** Министерство геологии Казахской ССР, Центрально-Казахстанское геолого-производственное объединение (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR, Central Kazakhstan Geological-Production Association)
- **Library stamp:** Казахская ССР, Республиканский геологический фонд, Инв. №10622 (Kazakh SSR Republican Geological Fund, Inventory No. 10622)
- **Drawing number:** Чертеж №20

The map covers a portion of northern/central Kazakhstan corresponding to the 1:200,000 topographic sheet N-42-XXXIII. The coverage area is irregular — the field data points do not fill the entire sheet, being concentrated primarily in the central, southern, and southwestern portions, with the northern and far eastern areas largely empty of data points.

---

## Key Content

**Point distribution:**
The map is densely populated with **circular symbols** (open/hollow circles of varying sizes) representing individual field data stations — most likely borehole locations, geophysical measurement stations, or sampling points. The circles vary slightly in size, which may indicate different types of observations or data categories (the legend is referenced on another drawing — "Условные обозначения см. на чертеже №12").

**Spatial patterns:**
- The highest density of data points is concentrated in a broad zone running from the **west-central to south-central** portion of the map, with particularly tight clustering in several sub-areas suggesting detailed local investigations or drill field campaigns.
- There is a clearly defined **northern boundary** to the data coverage, roughly horizontal, below which most data points appear — suggesting either a geological boundary, administrative survey limit, or the edge of a specific licensed block.
- A prominent **diagonal line** (or lines) crossing the southern portion of the map likely represents seismic profiles, survey traverses, or geological cross-section lines.
- Some **larger circles** in the central cluster area may indicate boreholes or key reference points of greater importance.
- A **dotted/dashed line** pattern visible in the southeastern portion may represent a survey boundary, geological contact, or geophysical profile line.

**Numerical labels:**
Every data point is labeled with an alphanumeric identifier. Labels include:
- Prefixed codes such as **О-** (e.g., О-48, О-46, О-47, О-50, О-51, О-110, О-120, О-130, О-137, О-153, О-163, О-170, О-179, О-277, О-330, О-378, О-862, О-881, О-882, О-883, О-890, О-897, О-898, О-862, О-866, О-870, О-872, О-873, О-880, О-802, О-800, О-846, О-831, О-8 series broadly)
- **Numerical codes without prefix** (e.g., 7987, 9092, 2951, 7853, 7920, 7850, 7803, 7855, 7835, 7888, 1100, 1178, 7855, 3425, 1233, 3300, 3380, 3425, 8029, 10101, 10309, etc.)
- **РМ- prefixed codes** (e.g., РМ/К0300, РМ/К0350, РМ2/К1730) visible in the south-central area, likely referring to seismic reflection points or geophysical profile markers
- **ІРҮТ/13570** and similar codes in the far southwest corner
- Codes beginning with **Б-** and **Д-** may also be present

The data point numbering systems suggest **multiple survey types** were combined on this single coverage map (e.g., О- series = one survey type, plain numbers = another type, РМ = seismic or geophysical stations).
